answer:
In Charles Comte's *Traité de Législation*, Volume II, Chapter 7, the "développement physique acquis en Amérique" (acquired physical development in America) refers to the observed variations in **stature, muscular strength, robustness, body proportions, and overall physical constitution** among indigenous American peoples (of "American," Malay, and Negro species or varieties) across different latitudes—from polar extremes to the equator.[^1] 

Comte critiques Montesquieu's theory that cold climates produce larger, stronger bodies while heat produces the opposite, using traveler accounts (e.g., La Pérouse, Humboldt, Cook) to argue the reverse pattern often holds: northern cold-climate groups like Eskimos (near 70°N) are small, weak, and poorly formed (stature ~4 feet); temperate-zone natives (e.g., Iroquois/Hurons ~45–50°N, Louisiana natives) are taller, upright, and vigorous; tropical/equatorial peoples (e.g., Caribs ~8–10°S, Amazonians at equator, Mbayas/Lenguas ~20–22°S) exhibit athletic builds, extreme endurance, and superior strength (statures 5'8"+, carrying 225–350 lbs in heat); while southern cold extremes like Tierra del Fuego (~52–53°S) natives are frail and miserable.[^2][^3][^4] Patagonians/Tehuelches (~36–45°S) buck polar expectations with colossal statures (~6'3" average).[^5] 

Comte notes these traits strengthen from poles toward tropics/temperate zones (analogous to vegetation), but exceptions across "species" lead him to downplay direct climate effects in favor of lifestyle, subsistence, and terrain.[^6]

[^1]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL II*, [Chapter 7 (translation)](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/07-du-developpement-physique-acquis-en-amerique-et-da/translation/).

[^2]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL II*, [paragraph](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/07-du-developpement-physique-acquis-en-amerique-et-da/translation/#p-8ecf40).

[^3]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL II*, [paragraph](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/07-du-developpement-physique-acquis-en-amerique-et-da/translation/#p-29b32e).

[^4]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL II*, [paragraph](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/07-du-developpement-physique-acquis-en-amerique-et-da/translation/#p-3d4de8).

[^5]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL II*, [paragraph](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/07-du-developpement-physique-acquis-en-amerique-et-da/translation/#p-38f187).

[^6]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL II*, [paragraph](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/07-du-developpement-physique-acquis-en-amerique-et-da/translation/#p-3a07f6).
